---
title: "Glossary: Swift, Apple APIs, Models, and Protocols"
---

This glossary is a reference index. The book also defines each term at its
first meaningful use, where its local design consequences are easier to see.

## Swift language

`actor`
: A reference type whose mutable state is isolated by Swift concurrency: at
  most one task touches that state at a time, and outside callers may need
  `await` to enter.

ARC
: Automatic Reference Counting, Swift and Objective-C's compiler-assisted
  strong-reference lifetime system.

`async`
: Marks a function that may suspend. It does not guarantee a background thread
  or make shared state thread-safe.

`AsyncStream<Element>`
: A generic asynchronous sequence whose producer yields values and whose
  consumer iterates them with `for await`.

attribute
: Metadata beginning with `@`, such as `@discardableResult` or `@MainActor`,
  that changes compiler checking or generated behavior.

`Binding<T>`
: A typed getter/setter pair exposed as mutable state to SwiftUI controls.

closure
: An anonymous function value that can capture variables from its surrounding
  scope. `{ $0.id == target }` uses `$0` for its first inferred parameter.

`Codable`
: The composition of Swift's `Encodable` and `Decodable` protocols.

computed property
: A property implemented by accessor code rather than a stored field.

continuation
: A handle representing a suspended `async` caller. Resuming it — exactly once —
  with a value or an error restarts the suspended code; it is the bridge from
  callback-style APIs into `async` functions.

copy-on-write
: Value semantics implemented by sharing storage until one copy mutates.

`deinit`
: Finalizer run when the last strong reference to a class instance is released.

`enum`
: A compiler-checked sum type. Cases may carry typed payloads, making it
  comparable to a tagged C union.

`Equatable`
: Protocol requiring equality comparison with `==`.

`extension`
: Adds methods or conformances to an existing type without subclassing it.

generic type
: A type parameterized by another type, such as `AsyncStream<Event>`.

`guard`
: An early-exit construct. `guard condition else { return }` guarantees that
  the condition is true for the remainder of the scope.

`init`
: A Swift initializer. Every stored property must be initialized before the
  value becomes usable.

`let`
: Declares an immutable binding or stored property.

`LocalizedError`
: Foundation protocol exposing a human-readable error description.

`@MainActor`
: Global-actor annotation isolating a declaration to Swift's main executor —
  the "main actor" that owns UI-visible state in this application.

`mutating`
: Allows a struct or enum method to modify `self`.

`nil`
: The absence case of an `Optional`; it is not an unchecked null pointer.

nil coalescing (`??`)
: Returns an optional's wrapped value or a fallback when it is `nil`.

`@Observable`
: Observation-framework macro that synthesizes change tracking used by SwiftUI.

optional chaining (`?.`)
: Calls a member only when the preceding optional contains a value.

`Optional<T>` / `T?`
: A sum type containing either `.some(T)` or `.none`.

`private(set)`
: Exposes a property getter more widely than its setter.

property wrapper
: A type attached to a property with `@Name` syntax that supplies the
  property's storage or access behavior; SwiftUI's `@State` is an example.

`protocol`
: A compiler-checked interface contract.

`Sendable`
: Concurrency protocol indicating that a value may cross isolation boundaries.
  It does not serialize mutable implementation state.

serial queue
: A Grand Central Dispatch queue (`DispatchQueue`) that runs submitted
  closures one at a time in submission order. Code that touches a resource
  only from one serial queue gets mutual exclusion without a separate lock.

`some Protocol`
: Opaque return type hiding one concrete type while preserving static typing.

`@State`
: SwiftUI property wrapper providing persistent framework-managed storage to a
  transient view value.

`struct`
: A Swift value type. Assignment conceptually copies the value.

`Task`
: Unit of Swift asynchronous work with cancellation and task-local context.

`throws`, `throw`, and `try`
: Swift's structured error-propagation mechanism.

trailing closure
: Syntax placing a function's final closure argument after the closing
  parenthesis.

`@unchecked Sendable`
: Manual promise that a type satisfies cross-isolation safety even though the
  compiler cannot prove it.

`Unmanaged<T>`
: Explicit retain/release interface for passing Swift object references through
  C APIs that do not participate in ARC.

`var`
: Declares a mutable binding or stored property.

`@ViewBuilder`
: Result builder used to compose declarative SwiftUI child expressions.

## Application and models

ASR
: Automatic Speech Recognition.

attention
: The transformer operation that moves information between sequence positions:
  each position's query is scored against every key, softmax turns the scores
  into weights, and the weighted sum of values becomes that position's new
  content.

attention head
: One of several parallel attention units inside a layer, each operating on
  its own slice of the position vector, so different heads can track different
  relationships.

attention KV cache
: Key-value cache retaining transformer attention projections from previous
  positions during autoregressive decoding, so each new position reads them
  instead of recomputing them.

AUHAL
: Audio Unit Hardware Abstraction Layer, the macOS Audio Unit that exposes
  hardware input/output devices to applications.

autoregressive
: Generating a sequence one token at a time, each conditioned on everything
  generated before it.

BF16
: Brain Floating Point, a 16-bit floating-point format that keeps float32's
  8-bit exponent with a shorter fraction. It is not interchangeable with FP16.

BLAS
: Basic Linear Algebra Subprograms, the standard interface to tuned
  matrix/vector routines; Apple's implementation lives in Accelerate.

checkpoint
: A saved set of trained model weights.

CLI
: Command-Line Interface.

dBFS
: Decibels relative to Full Scale, a logarithmic level measure in which 0 is
  the loudest representable sample and more negative is quieter.

DFT
: Discrete Fourier Transform; rewrites a finite sampled signal as a sum of
  sinusoids, giving the energy contributed by each frequency.

DTO
: Data Transfer Object.

embedding
: A learned numeric vector that represents a token, position, or other input
  inside the model.

FFT
: Fast Fourier Transform, a fast algorithm for computing the DFT.

FIFO
: First In, First Out.

FP16
: IEEE-754 binary16 half-precision floating-point format.

GELU
: Gaussian Error Linear Unit, a smooth nonlinear activation.

GPU
: Graphics Processing Unit.

GQA
: Grouped-Query Attention: more query heads than key/value heads, each group
  of query heads sharing one K/V head. It shrinks the KV cache (fourfold in
  this decoder) at a small quality cost.

greedy decoding
: Always choosing the single highest-scoring token (the argmax of the logits)
  instead of sampling, which makes generation deterministic.

kernel (GPU)
: A function compiled for the GPU and executed by every thread of a dispatch
  grid, each thread computing one output element.

logits
: The raw per-vocabulary-entry scores a model produces before a token is
  selected; argmax or softmax converts them into a choice.

mel scale
: A perceptual pitch scale, roughly logarithmic in frequency, on which equal
  steps sound equally far apart to a human listener.

MLX
: Apple's array and machine-learning framework for Apple silicon.

MLX-LM
: Language-model tooling built on MLX.

MPS
: Metal Performance Shaders, Apple's library of tuned GPU primitives.

MSL
: Metal Shading Language, the C++-like language Metal kernels are written in.

`MTLBuffer`
: Metal byte allocation visible to GPU commands and, depending on storage mode,
  the CPU.

`MTLCommandBuffer`
: Ordered collection of Metal commands submitted to a command queue.

PCM
: Pulse-Code Modulation: audio stored as a stream of integer samples taken at
  a fixed rate.

pipeline state
: A compiled, executable form of one GPU shader function. Creating it is
  expensive, so the runtime caches pipeline state objects per kernel.

prefill
: Processing all positions of an existing prompt in one batched pass, filling
  the KV cache, before one-token-at-a-time decoding begins.

RMS
: Root Mean Square, the square root of the mean of squared samples — a
  loudness measure.

RMSNorm
: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization.

RoPE
: Rotary Position Embedding; encodes position by rotating query/key vector
  pairs by position-dependent angles, so attention sees relative distance.

sample rate
: The number of audio measurements (samples) per second; this application
  converts capture to 16,000 samples per second.

SiLU
: Sigmoid Linear Unit activation.

SIMD group
: The hardware subdivision of a Metal threadgroup (32 threads on Apple GPUs)
  whose threads execute in lockstep and can exchange values directly.

softmax
: Normalizes a vector of scores into positive weights that sum to one, by
  exponentiating each entry and dividing by the total.

spectrogram
: A time-frequency picture of a signal: each column is one short time frame,
  each row the energy in one frequency band.

SPM
: Swift Package Manager.

STFT
: Short-Time Fourier Transform: a DFT applied to successive overlapping
  windows of a signal, producing a spectrogram.

temperature
: A sampling parameter that scales logits before a token is chosen; zero (or
  greedy decoding) always picks the most likely token.

tensor
: A multi-dimensional numeric array, the unit of model computation; its shape,
  such as `[sequence, dimension]`, is part of its meaning.

threadgroup
: A block of GPU threads dispatched together that share a small fast memory
  and can synchronize at a barrier; a dispatch grid is divided into
  threadgroups.

token
: A model-chosen unit of text, often a word fragment or byte sequence; the
  tokenizer maps token ids to bytes and back.

transformer
: A neural-network architecture built from stacked layers of attention and
  feed-forward blocks; both the Voxtral audio encoder and text decoder are
  transformers.

UI
: User Interface.

UUID
: Universally Unique Identifier.

WAV
: Waveform Audio File Format.

WER
: Word Error Rate: word insertions, deletions, and substitutions divided by
  the reference word count.

## Networking and formats

ABI
: Application Binary Interface.

API
: Application Programming Interface.

ASCII
: American Standard Code for Information Interchange.

Base64
: Binary-to-text encoding representing each 24 input bits as four printable
  characters, normally expanding data by roughly one third.

bearer token (capability)
: A secret random value whose possession alone authorizes access, with no
  account identity; the caption viewer and student URLs each embed one.

Bonjour
: Apple's zero-configuration networking: multicast DNS name lookup plus DNS
  Service Discovery on the local network.

DNS
: Domain Name System, the protocol that maps names to network addresses;
  Bonjour uses its multicast form on the local network.

endpoint
: A network address a client sends requests to — host and port, plus a path
  for HTTP.

`EventSource`
: The browser's built-in SSE client object; it opens the stream and
  automatically reconnects after interruptions.

HTTP
: Hypertext Transfer Protocol, the request/response protocol used by web
  clients and servers.

I/O
: Input/Output.

IPv4
: Internet Protocol version 4.

JSON
: JavaScript Object Notation.

little-endian
: Integer byte order storing the least significant byte first.

loopback
: The machine-internal network interface (127.0.0.1); traffic sent to it never
  leaves the computer.

`mmap`
: POSIX operation mapping file-backed bytes into a process's virtual address
  space.

NAT
: Network Address Translation: a router rewrites addresses so many devices
  share one outward address, which can make distinct clients appear to be one
  source.

PC
: Personal Computer.

POST
: HTTP request method conventionally used to submit data.

QR
: Quick Response code.

rate limiting
: Rejecting requests that exceed a configured count per time window. It
  protects availability; it does not prove identity.

RIFF
: Resource Interchange File Format, the chunk container used by WAV.

SSE
: Server-Sent Events: one HTTP response stays open and the server appends
  UTF-8 `event:`/`data:` blocks that the browser receives incrementally — a
  one-way push stream.

TCP
: Transmission Control Protocol, the reliable ordered byte-stream transport
  beneath HTTP.

URL
: Uniform Resource Locator.

`URLSession`
: Foundation's HTTP client. Requests run asynchronously; results arrive
  through completion callbacks, delegate methods, or `async` calls.

UTF-8
: Unicode Transformation Format, 8-bit form.

WebSocket
: HTTP-upgraded bidirectional framed transport over one connection.

Wi-Fi
: Wi-Fi Alliance brand name for IEEE 802.11 wireless networking. It is not an
  acronym for “Wireless Fidelity.”
